steps.title: Extract table values for age ≥ 10
steps.content: From the table, participants aged 10—19 total 30, and those aged 20+ total 30, giving 60 participants with age ≥ 10. Among these, Group A has 17 (ages 10—19) and 8 (ages 20+), totaling 25 from Group A.

steps.title: Calculate the conditional probability
steps.formula: $$P(A|B) = \frac{\text{Number in A and B}}{\text{Number in B}}$$
steps.content: P(Group A | age ≥ 10) = $\frac{25}{60} = \frac{5}{12}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
